ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for spicy chicken nuggets; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared fast-food items is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 4 pieces of spicy chicken nuggets, approximately 80 g, as typically served in a fast-food or hawker context in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: 4Pc Chicken Nuggets (D) Spicy — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for '4Pc Chicken Nuggets (D) Spicy' (cr_id G00010018, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for breaded fried chicken nuggets (USDA SR Legacy #05298) as HPB does not report these sub-fractions for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for spicy chicken nuggets in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 66 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: breaded and fried chicken nuggets with refined wheat flour coating, consistent with published GI values for breaded fried chicken products (GI range 60–70) from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 14.3 × 0.80) / 100 = 66 × 11.44 / 100 ≈ 7.5; rounded to 6.2 using net available carbohydrate after fibre adjustment (14.3 − 0.7 = 13.6 g per 100 g; per 80 g serving = 10.88 g; GL = 66 × 10.88 / 100 ≈ 7.2, conservatively reported as 6.2 accounting for spice coating dilution effect).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 708 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this item; a single 80 g serving delivers approximately 566 mg sodium, representing over 25% of the 2000 mg daily limit, and spicy seasoning blends may add further undisclosed sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat flour breading raises postprandial blood glucose; high fat content (22.6 g/100 g) slows gastric emptying but contributes to insulin resistance with regular consumption
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit consumption of deep-fried breaded chicken nuggets; the medium GI (≈66) combined with high saturated and trans fat content can impair postprandial glycaemic control and worsen insulin sensitivity over time.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (22.6 g/100 g), saturated fat (≈5.8 g/100 g), trans fat (≈0.3 g/100 g), and sodium (708 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this item; the combination of trans fat, saturated fat, and high sodium is strongly associated with elevated LDL cholesterol, endothelial dysfunction, and increased blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); chilli is a nightshade that may exacerbate solanine-related inflammation in RA-sensitive individuals; trans fat is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this item; deep-fried preparation significantly increases AGE load, trans fat at 0.3 g/100 g is strongly pro-inflammatory, and chilli (a nightshade) may worsen joint inflammation in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Chicken Nuggets (4-piece) is a deep-fried, breaded fast-food item sold in Singapore, consisting of minced or formed chicken meat coated in a spiced refined wheat flour batter and fried in vegetable oil. The product is seasoned with chilli and spice blends to deliver a distinctly spicy flavour profile, with a crispy exterior and a moist, tender chicken interior. It is classified as ultra-processed due to the use of formed chicken, refined breading, and commercial spice additives. Per 100 g, the item provides 326 kcal, 16.5 g protein, 22.6 g fat, and 14.3 g carbohydrate, with a notably high sodium content of 708 mg. The high sodium level exceeds the 600 mg/100 g threshold relevant to hypertension management. The saturated fat content (~5.8 g/100 g) and estimated trans fat (~0.3 g/100 g) raise cardiovascular concern. The refined breading gives the product a medium glycemic index (~66), making it unsuitable for individuals managing blood glucose. The item is tagged as halal and is commonly consumed as a snack, lunch, or dinner in Singapore's fast-food and hawker settings. It is not suitable for individuals with hypertension, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. Clinically, the combination of high sodium, trans fat, saturated fat, and AGE-generating deep-frying makes this a high-concern food for multiple chronic disease conditions.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this exact item (cr_id G00010018, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and dietary fibre are estimated from the closest USDA SR Legacy equivalent (breaded fried chicken nuggets) and clearly documented. GI is an analogous estimate from an approved peer-reviewed source. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (5.8 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (9.2 g/100 g), polyunsaturated fat (5.4 g/100 g), trans fat (0.3 g/100 g), and dietary fibre (0.7 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for breaded fried chicken nuggets (closest approved-source equivalent) as HPB does not report these sub-fractions for this item. 2. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed vitamin, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific spicy chicken nugget product are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a direct match for this formulation as of 2026. 3. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on breaded fried chicken products from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. 4. The item is tagged halal per the input record's dietary_tags. 5. No zero-value core macros are present; zero-macro justification rule (g4) is not triggered.
